checkpoint_state: post-lambda / post-sigma-b / post-safe-phi-b / post-upsilon-a / post-phi-b3 / post-upsilon-b / post-upsilon-c / post-phi-b4 / pre-phi-b5-or-other-next-block

distributed_authority_reconsideration.summary: The repo now has enough runtime and control-plane law to judge distributed authority honestly, but not enough maturity to reopen Φ-B5 directly.

final_control_plane_maturity_before_phi_.summary: A final narrow Υ-D band is still the safer next block because live trust execution, revocation propagation, live-cutover receipt continuity, and publication/trust operationalization remain unfinished.

zeta_blocker_reduction.summary: Φ-B4 closes the hotswap-boundary ambiguity, but Ζ still remains blocked by distributed authority, replication proof, live trust rotation, live revocation continuity, and live-cutover continuity gaps.

ambiguities_eliminated: finishing hotswap doctrine does not automatically make distributed authority ready
replication policy substrate does not equal lawful authority handoff
trust verification tooling does not equal live trust-rotation maturity

phi_b5_readiness.title: DISTRIBUTED_AUTHORITY_FOUNDATIONS-0
phi_b5_readiness.summary: Distributed authority is now reconsiderable in theory, but remains blocked on live trust execution maturity, revocation propagation continuity, live-cutover receipt generalization, publication/trust operationalization, and still-missing authority handoff, quorum, and deterministic replication proof.

further_upsilon_readiness.title: LIVE_TRUST_ROTATION_AND_REVOCATION_PROPAGATION_PREREQUISITES-0
further_upsilon_readiness.summary: Trust execution and revocation continuity are explicit, and Φ-B4 now cleanly marks trust-sensitive live transitions as outside current runtime-safe boundaries.

further_upsilon_readiness.title: LIVE_CUTOVER_RECEIPTS_AND_RUNTIME_CONTINUITY-0
further_upsilon_readiness.summary: Receipt and provenance law can now be generalized for live-cutover-adjacent continuity without letting release-control convenience redefine runtime meaning.

further_upsilon_readiness.title: PUBLICATION_AND_TRUST_OPERATIONALIZATION_ALIGNMENT-0
further_upsilon_readiness.summary: Publication/trust gates and trust continuity doctrine are explicit enough to align operational maturity, but the work must remain doctrinal and review-aware.

further_upsilon_readiness.title: LIVE_RELEASE_CONTROLLER_AND_AUTOMATION_ALIGNMENT-0
further_upsilon_readiness.summary: Release-controller execution posture is already materially embodied and is no longer the main blocker reducer before Φ-B5.

zeta_blockers_or_prerequisites.summary: Authority handoff, distributed replay verification, proof-anchor quorum, and shard relocation semantics remain unresolved.
Replication policy substrate exists, but deterministic replication proof and state-partition transfer proof do not.
Trust roots remain empty and trust policies remain provisional.
Revocation continuity is explicit, but live propagation and runtime-facing trust transition choreography remain unproven.
Receipts are explicit for release/control transactions, but not yet generalized across live-cutover runtime continuity.
Gates and trust doctrine exist, but live publication/trust execution posture remains review-heavy and unimplemented.
Hotswap law is explicit now, but runtime cutover proof still remains unproven in operation.
Nothing yet proves distributed trust acceptance, revocation behavior, and authority convergence under handoff.

must_avoid_replacing: runtime boundary law with control-plane folklore
trust truth with mirror or archive visibility
checkpoint authority with stale planning drift

ownership_projection_cautions: fields/ remains canonical semantic field substrate while field/ remains transitional
schema/ remains canonical semantic contract law while schemas/ remains projection-facing
packs/ remains canonical for runtime packaging and activation scope while data/packs/ remains scoped authored-pack authority
canonical prose and semantic specs outrank projected or generated mirrors
the thin runtime/ root is not canonical by name alone
release, archive, mirror, publication, trust, and tooling convenience must not infer canon or permission
older planning numbering drift remains evidence rather than authority

final_verdict.reason: Φ-B4 closes the runtime boundary ambiguity that previously kept distributed authority premature, but the repo still needs one final live trust and live-cutover continuity maturity band before Φ-B5 becomes a safe next move.

notes_on_identifier_policy: Stable family identifiers remain preferred over stale older Υ numbering in legacy planning mirrors.
Υ-D labels are now intentionally carried forward as the active names for the final narrow maturity band before any reconsideration of Φ-B5.
